from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of Triple objects about universities (or related entities)
    into a single fluent sentence with proper grammatical structure.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping from predicate to a readable phrase template.
    templates = {
        "city": "{subject} is located in {object}",
        "country": "{subject} is in {object}",
        "latinName": "{subject}'s Latin name is {object}",
        "nickname": "{subject}'s nickname is {object}",
        "rector": "{subject}'s rector is {object}",
        "academicStaffSize": "{subject} has an academic staff of {object}",
        "established": "{subject} was established in {object}",
        "state": "{subject} is in the state of {object}",
        "governmentType": "{subject} has a government type of {object}",
        "hasToItsNortheast": "{subject} has to its northeast {object}",
        "dean": "{subject}'s dean is {object}",
        "location": "{subject} is located in {object}",
        "numberOfStudents": "{subject} has {object} students",
        "affiliation": "{subject} is affiliated with {object}",
        "director": "{subject}'s director is {object}",
        "motto": "{subject}'s motto is \"{object}\"",
        "numberOfPostgraduateStudents": "{subject} has {object} postgraduate students",
        "numberOfUndergraduateStudents": "{subject} has {object} undergraduate students",
        "officialSchoolColour": "{subject}'s official school colour is {object}",
        "outlookRanking": "{subject} is ranked {object} in the Outlook ranking",
        "sportsOffered": "{subject} offers the sport {object}",
        "wasGivenTheTechnicalCampusStatusBy": "{subject} was given the technical campus status by {object}",
        "founder": "{subject} was founded by {object}",
        "leader": "{subject} is led by {object}",
        "leaderTitle": "{subject}'s leader title is {object}",
        "religion": "{subject}'s religion is {object}",
        "headquarter": "{subject}'s headquarter is {object}",
        "largestCity": "{subject}'s largest city is {object}",
        "river": "{subject}'s river is {object}",
        "hasToItsWest": "{subject} has to its west {object}",
        "anthem": "{subject}'s anthem is \"{object}\"",
        "ethnicGroup": "{subject}'s ethnic group includes {object}",
        "legislature": "{subject}'s legislature is {object}",
        "sportGoverningBody": "{subject}'s sport governing body is {object}",
        "isPartOf": "{subject} is part of {object}",
        "neighboringMunicipality": "{subject} neighbors {object}",
        "capital": "{subject}'s capital is {object}",
        "patronSaint": "{subject}'s patron saint is {object}",
        "hasToItsNorthwest": "{subject} has to its northwest {object}",
        "campus": "{subject}'s campus is located at {object}",
        "president": "{subject}'s president is {object}",
        "staff": "{subject} has {object} staff members",
        "numberOfDoctoralStudents": "{subject} has {object} doctoral students",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "{subject} is at an elevation of {object} meters above sea level",
        "postalCode": "{subject}'s postal code is {object}",
        "longName": "{subject}'s long name is {object}",
    }

    # Group triples by subject
    subject_triples = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_triples:
            subject_triples[t.subject] = []
        subject_triples[t.subject].append(t)

    # Find the main subject (prefer university-related subjects)
    main_subject = None
    for subj in subject_triples.keys():
        if "University" in subj or "College" in subj or "Institute" in subj:
            main_subject = subj
            break
    if not main_subject and subject_triples:
        main_subject = list(subject_triples.keys())[0]

    # Collect all triples for the main subject
    main_triples = subject_triples.get(main_subject, [])

    # Build templates for different predicate types with better phrasing
    predicate_templates = {
        "city": "located in {object}",
        "country": "in {object}",
        "latinName": "Latin name is {object}",
        "nickname": "nickname is {object}",
        "rector": "rector is {object}",
        "academicStaffSize": "has an academic staff of {object}",
        "established": "was established in {object}",
        "state": "in the state of {object}",
        "governmentType": "has a government type of {object}",
        "hasToItsNortheast": "has to its northeast {object}",
        "dean": "dean is {object}",
        "location": "located in {object}",
        "numberOfStudents": "has {object} students",
        "affiliation": "affiliated with {object}",
        "director": "director is {object}",
        "motto": "motto is \"{object}\"",
        "numberOfPostgraduateStudents": "has {object} postgraduate students",
        "numberOfUndergraduateStudents": "has {object} undergraduate students",
        "officialSchoolColour": "official school colour is {object}",
        "outlookRanking": "ranked {object} in the Outlook ranking",
        "sportsOffered": "offers the sport {object}",
        "wasGivenTheTechnicalCampusStatusBy": "was given the technical campus status by {object}",
        "founder": "founded by {object}",
        "leader": "led by {object}",
        "leaderTitle": "leader title is {object}",
        "religion": "religion is {object}",
        "headquarter": "headquarter is {object}",
        "largestCity": "largest city is {object}",
        "river": "river is {object}",
        "hasToItsWest": "has to its west {object}",
        "anthem": "anthem is \"{object}\"",
        "ethnicGroup": "ethnic group includes {object}",
        "legislature": "legislature is {object}",
        "sportGoverningBody": "sport governing body is {object}",
        "isPartOf": "part of {object}",
        "neighboringMunicipality": "neighbors {object}",
        "capital": "capital is {object}",
        "patronSaint": "patron saint is {object}",
        "hasToItsNorthwest": "has to its northwest {object}",
        "campus": "campus is located at {object}",
        "president": "president is {object}",
        "staff": "has {object} staff members",
        "numberOfDoctoralStudents": "has {object} doctoral students",
        "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": "at an elevation of {object} meters above sea level",
        "postalCode": "postal code is {object}",
        "longName": "long name is {object}",
    }

    # Build clauses for the main subject, using relative clauses to avoid repetition
    main_clauses = []
    location_clauses = []  # For country, city, state, location
    other_clauses = []

    for t in main_triples:
        tmpl = predicate_templates.get(t.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            clause = tmpl.format(object=t.object)
            # Prioritize location predicates for relative clause placement
            if t.predicate in ["city", "country", "state", "location"]:
                location_clauses.append(clause)
            else:
                other_clauses.append(clause)

    # Also collect clauses from related subjects (country, state, city, etc.)
    related_subject_clauses = []
    for subj, triples_list in subject_triples.items():
        if subj != main_subject:
            for t in triples_list:
                tmpl = predicate_templates.get(t.predicate)
                if tmpl:
                    clause = tmpl.format(object=t.object)
                    related_subject_clauses.append(f"{subj} {clause}")

    # Build the sentence with proper structure
    if not main_clauses and not location_clauses and not other_clauses:
        if triples:
            t = triples[0]
            tmpl = predicate_templates.get(t.predicate, "{predicate} {object}")
            if tmpl:
                sentence = f"{t.subject} {tmpl.format(object=t.object)}"
            else:
                sentence = f"{t.subject} {t.predicate} {t.object}"
        else:
            return ""
    else:
        # Start with subject
        sentence_parts = [main_subject]

        # Add location information as a relative clause if available
        if location_clauses:
            loc_text = ", ".join(location_clauses)
            sentence_parts.append(f" which is {loc_text}")

        # Add other information
        if other_clauses:
            if len(other_clauses) == 1:
                sentence_parts.append(f" and {other_clauses[0]}")
            else:
                last = other_clauses[-1]
                middle = ", ".join(other_clauses[:-1])
                if middle:
                    sentence_parts.append(f", {middle}, and {last}")
                else:
                    sentence_parts.append(f" and {last}")

        sentence = "".join(sentence_parts)

    # Ensure the sentence ends with a period and starts with a capital letter
    if sentence:
        sentence = sentence.rstrip()
        if not sentence.endswith('.'):
            sentence = sentence + "."
        sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

An incorrect diff format was detected in this change:


Looking at the low score examples, I can see the main issues:

1. The program only processes triples for the main subject (university) and ignores related entities (country, state, etc.)
2. The generated sentences don't incorporate ALL the triples into one complex sentence
3. Information about related entities (like country's river, state's leader) is being lost

The key improvement is to process triples from ALL subjects and create a truly complex sentence that links the university information with related entity information.
